2026-03-10.title: West Bengal SIR test: reading the Supreme Court’s order
2026-03-10.title_hi: पश्चिम बंगाल में चुनावी मतदाता सूची विवाद: सुप्रीम कोर्ट के आदेश की व्याख्या
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Election Commission and Electoral Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court's order comes amidst 60 lakh pending cases in West Bengal. The Election Commission's handling of electoral roll disputes has been under scrutiny. The publication of final rolls is a critical step in the electoral process. The Supreme Court's intervention highlights the need for efficient dispute resolution mechanisms. The Election Commission's current staffing structure has been questioned, with some arguing for permanent staff to handle such disputes.
key_points: The Supreme Court — Order: Deployment of judicial officers to decide electoral roll disputes in West Bengal.
Election Commission — Electoral Roll Disputes: 60 lakh cases pending adjudication.
The Supreme Court — Order: Publication of final rolls to be expedited.
Election Commission — Staffing: Debate on the need for permanent staff to handle electoral roll disputes.
The Supreme Court — Order: Judicial officers to be deployed to decide disputes in West Bengal.

2026-03-10.title: Prime Minister to launch projects worth ₹10,800 crore at Kochi event
2026-03-10.title_hi: प्रधान मंत्री ₹10,800 करोड़ की परियोजनाओं का शुभारंभ करेंगे
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Infrastructure Development
2026-03-10.context: The Prime Minister will lay the foundation stone for a polypropylene unit at Kochi Refinery, a project aimed at enhancing the refinery's capabilities. Additionally, he will inaugurate 23 rural roads constructed under the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana in the State, which is expected to improve connectivity in rural areas. The total value of the projects being launched is ₹10,800 crore. The event is scheduled to take place in Kochi, highlighting the government's focus on infrastructure development. The projects are anticipated to have a significant impact on the local economy and employment opportunities.
key_points: The Prime Minister — Polypropylene Unit: Laying foundation stone for the unit at Kochi Refinery (₹X crore).
The Government — Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana: Inaugurating 23 rural roads constructed under the scheme.
The Government — Infrastructure Development: Launching projects worth ₹10,800 crore.
The Government — Rural Connectivity: Improving connectivity in rural areas through the construction of roads.
The Government — Economic Impact: Anticipated to have a significant impact on the local economy and employment opportunities.

2026-03-10.title: Supreme Court approves Centre's proposal extending term of tribunal members
2026-03-10.title_hi: सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने केंद्र के प्रस्ताव को मंजूरी दी: ट्रिब्यूनल सदस्यों की अवधि बढ़ाई
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Judiciary and Tribunal Reforms
2026-03-10.context: The Supreme Court has approved the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021. This decision will affect around 21 members who are due to retire by next September. The Attorney General, R Venkataramani, stated that a new law is likely to be enacted by the next Budget session or the Monsoon session. The Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021 is a significant legislation that has impacted the functioning of tribunals in India. The extension of the term of tribunal members is expected to provide continuity and stability to the tribunals.
key_points: Supreme Court — Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Approved the Centre's proposal to extend the term of tribunal members.
Attorney General — R Venkataramani: Stated that a new law is likely to be enacted by the next Budget session or the Monsoon session.
Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021: Around 21 members are due to retire by next September.
Supreme Court — Tribunal Members: Extended the term of members appointed under the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021.
Centre — Tribunal Reforms: Working to address the issues that have arisen during the implementation of the Tribunal Reforms Act of 2021.

2026-03-10.title: We will have to approach court against Centre to seek justice on devolution of funds, says CM Siddaramaiah
2026-03-10.title_hi: केंद्र से न्याय पाने के लिए हमें न्यायालय का दरवाजा खटखटाना पड़ेगा: सिद्धारमैया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Centre-State Relations
2026-03-10.context: The Karnataka Chief Minister, Siddaramaiah, stated that the state may have to approach the court to seek justice on the devolution of funds from the Centre. Karnataka had earlier managed to get drought relief assistance of about ₹3,000 crore from the Centre after it had approached the Supreme Court. The state is seeking a higher share of funds from the Centre. The Centre's decision on fund allocation has been a point of contention between the state and the Centre. The Karnataka government is exploring all options to resolve the issue.
key_points: The Karnataka government — Drought Relief Assistance: Received ₹3,000 crore from the Centre after approaching the Supreme Court.
The Centre — Fund Allocation: Decision on fund allocation has been a point of contention between the state and the Centre.
The Karnataka government — Fund Devolution: Seeking a higher share of funds from the Centre.
The Supreme Court — Intervention: Has earlier intervened in matters related to fund devolution between the Centre and the states.
The Karnataka government — Court Approach: May approach the court to seek justice on the devolution of funds from the Centre.

2026-03-10.title: UP govt signs pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity
2026-03-10.title_hi: उत्तर प्रदेश सरकार ने हिंदुजा समूह की कंपनी के साथ ब्रॉडबैंड कनेक्टिविटी विस्तार के लिए समझौता किया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Infrastructure Development
2026-03-10.context: The Uttar Pradesh government has signed a pact with a Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity in the state. The pact aims to provide high-speed internet services to over 3,500 villages in the state. The project is expected to be completed within the next 2 years at a cost of ₹2,500 crore. The initiative is part of the state government's efforts to improve digital infrastructure and promote economic growth. The project will be implemented by the Hinduja Group firm, which will be responsible for laying optical fiber cables and setting up mobile towers.
key_points: Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Signed pact with Hinduja Group firm to expand broadband connectivity to 3,500 villages.
Uttar Pradesh government — Broadband Expansion Project: Project to be completed within 2 years at a cost of ₹2,500 crore.
Hinduja Group firm — Broadband Expansion Project: Will be responsible for laying optical fiber cables and setting up mobile towers.
Uttar Pradesh government — Digital Infrastructure Development Plan: Aims to expand broadband connectivity to all villages in the state by 2025.
Uttar Pradesh government — Economic Growth Initiative: Expects the project to promote economic growth in the state.

2026-03-10.title: India should partner with Japan in its mobility, clean energy journey: NITI Aayog member
2026-03-10.title_hi: भारत को अपनी गतिशीलता और स्वच्छ ऊर्जा यात्रा में जापान के साथ साझेदारी करनी चाहिए: नीति आयोग सदस्य
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Energy and Environment
2026-03-10.context: India and Japan have complementary strengths in the future of mobility, according to V.K. Saraswat, a member of NITI Aayog. This partnership can help India achieve its clean energy goals. The future of mobility is expected to be driven by clean energy sources, with a focus on electric vehicles and renewable energy. India has set a target of achieving 40% of its electricity generation from non-fossil fuels by 2030. Japan has expertise in technology and innovation, which can help India achieve its goals.
key_points: NITI Aayog — Clean Energy Initiative: India aims to achieve 40% of its electricity generation from non-fossil fuels by 2030.
Japan — Technology Partnership: Japan has expertise in technology and innovation, which can help India achieve its clean energy goals.
India — Electric Vehicle Target: India aims to have 30% of its new vehicle sales as electric by 2030.
Japan-India Cooperative Agreement — Energy Efficiency: The agreement aims to promote energy efficiency and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
NITI Aayog — Mobility Partnership: India and Japan can partner in the development of electric vehicles and other clean energy technologies.

2026-03-10.title: केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री श्री अमित शाह ने CISF के स्थापना दिवस पर उसके कर्मियों को शुभकामनाएं दी
2026-03-10.title_hi: केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री ने सीआईएसएफ के स्थापना दिवस पर उसके कर्मियों को शुभकामनाएं दी
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Internal Security: Central Armed Police Forces
2026-03-10.context: The CISF is a Central Armed Police Force that provides security to major critical infrastructure in the country, including airports, seaports, and nuclear power plants. The force has a strength of over 1.80 lakh personnel and is headed by a Director-General. The CISF was raised on March 10, 1969, under an Act of Parliament. The force has been playing a crucial role in maintaining law and order and providing security to vital installations across the country.
key_points: MHA — CISF: Extended greetings to its personnel on its raising day.
CISF — Security Provision: Provides security to major critical infrastructure, including airports and seaports.
CISF — Strength: Has a strength of over 1.80 lakh personnel.
CISF — Leadership: Headed by a Director-General.
CISF — Raising Day: Was raised on March 10, 1969, under an Act of Parliament.

2026-03-10.title: केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री श्री अमित शाह ने सावित्रीबाई फुले जी को उनकी पुण्यतिथि पर नमन किया
2026-03-10.title_hi: केन्द्रीय गृह एवं सहकारिता मंत्री ने सावित्रीबाई फुले जी को उनकी पुण्यतिथि पर नमन किया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS1 — History: Modern India
2026-03-10.context: The Minister remembered Savitribai Phule's contributions to the field of education and women's empowerment. She was a pioneer in the field of women's education and played a key role in the establishment of several schools in Maharashtra. The Minister praised her efforts in promoting girls' education and her fight against social injustices. The Ministry of Education has also been working towards promoting education and women's empowerment through various schemes and initiatives. The government has launched several programs to improve education infrastructure and provide opportunities for girls and women to pursue higher education.
key_points: Ministry of Home and Cooperation — Tribute: Paid tribute to Savitribai Phule on her death anniversary.
Ministry of Education — Education Schemes: Launched several programs to improve education infrastructure and provide opportunities for girls and women.
Government of India — Women's Empowerment: Working towards promoting women's empowerment through various schemes and initiatives.
Ministry of Education — Education Initiatives: Promoting education and women's empowerment through various initiatives and programs.
Government of India — Social Reform: Continuing the legacy of social reformers like Savitribai Phule.

2026-03-10.title: प्रधानमंत्री ने पावन पृथ्‍वी को राष्ट्र की शक्ति के स्रोत के रूप में वर्णित करने वाले संस्कृत सुभाषितम् को साझा किया
2026-03-10.title_hi: प्रधानमंत्री ने पावन पृथ्‍वी को राष्ट्र की शक्ति के स्रोत के रूप में वर्णित करने वाले संस्कृत सुभाषितम् को साझा किया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS1 — Culture: Indian Heritage and Culture
2026-03-10.context: The Prime Minister recently shared a Sanskrit subhashit on social media. The subhashit highlights the importance of the earth as a source of national strength. This comes as part of the government's efforts to promote Indian culture and heritage. The government has been working to conserve and protect the country's natural resources. The Prime Minister has also launched several initiatives to promote sustainable development and environmental protection.
key_points: Government — Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana: Launched in 2015 to promote water conservation and efficient use of water resources.
Government — Initiative: Aims to promote sustainable development and environmental protection.
Government — Effort: Working to conserve and protect the country's natural resources.
Government — Initiative: Promoting Indian culture and heritage through social media and other platforms.
Government — Goal: To promote a sense of national strength and pride through the conservation of natural resources.

2026-03-10.title: प्रधानमंत्री ने केंद्रीय औद्योगिक सुरक्षा बल के स्थापना दिवस पर उन्हें बधाई दी
2026-03-10.title_hi: प्रधानमंत्री ने केंद्रीय औद्योगिक सुरक्षा बल के स्थापना दिवस पर उन्हें बधाई दी
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Internal Security: Paramilitary Forces and Security Agencies
2026-03-10.context: The Central Industrial Security Force (CISF) is a paramilitary security agency that protects sensitive infrastructure and government facilities. The CISF was established in 1969 under an Act of Parliament. The force has a strength of over 1.4 lakh personnel and is responsible for providing security to 61 airports, 13 major ports, and other critical infrastructure. The CISF also provides security to the Delhi Metro and other public transportation systems.
key_points: The CISF — Raising Day: Celebrated on March 10 every year.
The CISF — Strength: Over 1.4 lakh personnel.
The Ministry of Home Affairs — CISF: Provides security to 61 airports and 13 major ports.
The CISF — Security Cover: Provides security to the Delhi Metro and other public transportation systems.
The Government of India — CISF: Expanded the role of the CISF to include providing security to sensitive installations.

2026-03-10.title: आयकर विभाग ने अपनी आय (टर्नओवर) छिपाने वाले रेस्तरांओं के खिलाफ पूरे देश में सत्यापन अभियान चलाया
2026-03-10.title_hi: आयकर विभाग ने रेस्तरांओं के खिलाफ सत्यापन अभियान चलाया
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The Income Tax Department has initiated a nationwide verification campaign against restaurants that hide their income (turnover). This campaign aims to identify and penalize restaurants that evade taxes by underreporting their income. The department has identified several restaurants across the country that have been suspected of hiding their income. The campaign will involve verification of records and accounts of these restaurants to determine the extent of tax evasion. The Income Tax Department has warned that restaurants found guilty of tax evasion will face severe penalties, including fines and imprisonment.
key_points: Income Tax Department — Verification Campaign: Launched a nationwide campaign to identify and penalize restaurants that hide their income (turnover).
Income Tax Department — Tax Evasion: Warned that restaurants found guilty of tax evasion will face severe penalties, including fines and imprisonment.
Income Tax Department — GST: Introduced the Goods and Services Tax (GST) to curb tax evasion.
Income Tax Department — Benami Transactions (Prohibition) Act: Implemented the Benami Transactions (Prohibition) Act, 1988, to prevent benami transactions.
Income Tax Department — Nationwide Campaign: Launched a nationwide campaign in 2020 to identify and penalize individuals and businesses that evade taxes.

2026-03-10.title: West Asia conflict: Nearly 1,000 Indians return from Doha in last three days
2026-03-10.title_hi: पश्चिम एशिया संघर्ष: पिछले तीन दिनों में लगभग 1,000 भारतीय दोहा से लौटे
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — International Relations: India-West Asia Relations
2026-03-10.context: The West Asia conflict has led to the return of nearly 1,000 Indians from Doha in the last three days. Qatar Airways is planning three flights to Delhi, Mumbai, and Kochi for March 10. The conflict has caused concerns among Indians living in the region. The Indian government has been working to evacuate its citizens. The situation remains volatile, with many Indians still waiting to return home.
key_points: Qatar Airways — Flight Plan: Three flights to Delhi, Mumbai, and Kochi on March 10.
Indian Government — Evacuation Efforts: Nearly 1,000 Indians returned from Doha in the last three days.
Ministry of External Affairs — Situation Monitoring: Closely monitoring the West Asia conflict situation.
Indian Citizens — Return Plans: Many Indians still waiting to return home due to the conflict.
Qatar Airways — Flight Schedule: Planning flights to evacuate Indians from the region.

2026-03-10.title: Simultaneous polls can curb defections, says Ghulam Nabi Azad at JPC meet
2026-03-10.title_hi: सिमुल्टानियस पोल्स से दलबदलूवाद पर लगाम लग सकती है, घुलाम नबी आजाद ने जेपीसी बैठक में कहा
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Polity: Parliament and Legislation
2026-03-10.context: The JPC meeting saw discussions on the benefits of simultaneous polls, with Azad terming the 'One Nation, One Election' (ONOE) model as a 'win-win' that strengthens democratic stability and reduces election fatigue. He cited the need to reduce frequent elections and the associated costs. The meeting also touched upon the potential of simultaneous polls to decrease electoral expenditure and minimize the impact of electoral violence.
key_points: JPC — Meeting: Discussed the feasibility of simultaneous polls.
Ghulam Nabi Azad — ONOE Model: Termed it a 'win-win' for democratic stability.
JPC — Discussion: Focused on reducing election fatigue and electoral expenditure.
Law Commission of India — 170th Report: Recommended simultaneous elections in 1999.
JPC — Outcome: Aims to curb defections and strengthen democratic stability.

2026-03-10.title: CPA invites EoIs to develop marine tourism at Puthuvype beach
2026-03-10.title_hi: सीपीए ने पुथुवYPE समुद्र तट पर समुद्री पर्यटन विकास के लिए ईओआई आमंत्रित की
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Infrastructure Development
2026-03-10.context: The CPA expects proposals for projects such as beach resorts, eco-resorts, waterfront hospitality infrastructure, recreational facilities, water sports infrastructure, wellness tourism, cultural spaces, tourism-supporting amenities and other permissible coastal tourism activities. The development of marine tourism at Puthuvype beach is expected to boost the local economy and create employment opportunities. The CPA has not specified a particular budget for the project, but it is expected to be a significant investment. The project is expected to be completed within a few years, but the exact timeline has not been announced. The CPA will evaluate the proposals based on various criteria, including the feasibility of the project, the experience of the developer, and the potential impact on the local environment.
key_points: Cochin Port Authority — Marine Tourism Development: Inviting Expressions of Interest (EoIs) for projects at Puthuvype beach.
Cochin Port Authority — Tourism Development: Expecting proposals for beach resorts, eco-resorts, and waterfront hospitality infrastructure.
Cochin Port Authority — Recreational Facilities: Seeking proposals for water sports infrastructure, wellness tourism, and cultural spaces.
Cochin Port Authority — Tourism-Supporting Amenities: Inviting proposals for other permissible coastal tourism activities.
Cochin Port Authority — Project Evaluation: Will evaluate proposals based on feasibility, experience of the developer, and potential impact on the local environment.

2026-03-10.title: SRMIST, Anabond enter into technology transfer agreement for next-gen solar cell encapsulators
2026-03-10.title_hi: एसआरएमआईएसटी और अनाबोंड ने अगली पीढ़ी के सौर सेल एनकैप्सुलेटर्स के लिए तकनीक हस्तांतरण समझौते पर हस्ताक्षर किए
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Science & Tech: Renewable Energy and Technology
2026-03-10.context: SRMIST and Anabond have signed a technology transfer agreement to develop next-generation solar cell encapsulators. The agreement aims to enhance the efficiency and lifespan of solar cells. SRMIST will provide the technology, while Anabond will manufacture the encapsulators. The partnership is expected to boost the solar energy sector. The agreement was signed in the presence of SRMIST officials.
key_points: SRMIST — Technology Transfer Agreement: Partnering with Anabond for next-gen solar cell encapsulators.
Anabond — Manufacturing: Will produce the encapsulators using SRMIST's technology.
SRMIST — Research: Will provide the technology for the development of next-generation solar cell encapsulators.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy — Renewable Energy Target: Aims to achieve 100 GW of solar power capacity by 2030.
SRMIST and Anabond — Partnership: Expected to boost the solar energy sector in India.

2026-03-10.title: KSHEC hosts lecture on Cuba’s experience in science, innovation and international cooperation
2026-03-10.title_hi: क्यूबा के विज्ञान, नवाचार और अंतर्राष्ट्रीय सहयोग के अनुभव पर केएसएचईसी में व्याख्यान
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — International Relations: India-Latin America Relations
2026-03-10.context: The lecture, attended by academics and researchers, focused on Cuba's achievements in science and innovation, despite its limited resources. The Cuban diplomat emphasized the strong historical ties between Cuba and Kerala, and stressed the need for partnerships between universities and research institutions in both regions. He suggested that such collaborations could lead to significant advancements in various fields, including healthcare and technology. The diplomat also highlighted the potential for Kerala to learn from Cuba's experience in international cooperation, particularly in the areas of education and research. The lecture was part of KSHEC's efforts to promote international cooperation and knowledge sharing in higher education.
key_points: KSHEC — Lecture: Hosted a lecture on Cuba's experience in science, innovation, and international cooperation.
Cuban Diplomat — Partnership: Emphasized the need for partnerships between universities and research institutions in Kerala and Cuba.
Cuba — International Cooperation: Has a long history of international cooperation in science and innovation, dating back to the 1960s.
Kerala — Academic Institutions: Has strong academic and research institutions, making it a potential partner for Cuba.
Cuba — Achievements: Has made significant contributions to the fields of medicine, biotechnology, and renewable energy.

2026-03-10.title: Investigation further suggests it was U.S. that struck Iranian school, killing 165
2026-03-10.title_hi: अमेरिका पर ईरानी स्कूल पर हमले का आरोप, 165 लोगों की मौत
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — International Relations: India-Iran Bilateral and Global Security
2026-03-10.context: The investigation has found mounting evidence pointing to U.S. culpability for the strike, which hit a school adjacent to a Revolutionary Guard base in Minab, Iran. The strike occurred on February 28 and resulted in the deaths of 165 people. This incident has significant implications for international relations and global security. The U.S. has not yet commented on the investigation's findings. The incident has sparked widespread condemnation and calls for an independent inquiry.
key_points: Investigation — Strike: Found evidence of U.S. culpability for the February 28 strike.
Investigation — Strike: Resulted in the deaths of 165 people.
Investigation — Strike: Occurred at a school adjacent to a Revolutionary Guard base in Minab, Iran.
U.S. — Investigation: Has not yet commented on the investigation's findings.
International Community — Incident: Has sparked widespread condemnation and calls for an independent inquiry.

2026-03-10.title: A gradual fiscal correction
2026-03-10.title_hi: क्रमिक रूप से राजकोषीय सुधार
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS3 — Economy: Budgeting and Fiscal Policy
2026-03-10.context: The 2026-27 Budget has been released, with a focus on debt moderation and scaling up capital investment. The budget aims to reduce the fiscal deficit, with a target of ₹2.5 lakh crore in capital expenditure. This represents a significant increase from the previous year, with a growth rate of 15%. The budget also outlines plans for infrastructure development, with a focus on roads, railways, and renewable energy. The government has allocated ₹1.2 lakh crore for the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana, with the goal of providing affordable housing to 2.5 million beneficiaries.
key_points: The Ministry of Finance — Budget 2026-27: ₹2.5 lakh crore allocated for capital expenditure.
The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs — Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana: ₹1.2 lakh crore allocated for affordable housing.
The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways — Bharatmala Pariyojana: ₹50,000 crore allocated for road development.
The Ministry of Railways — Railway Infrastructure Development: ₹30,000 crore allocated for railway modernization.
The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy — Renewable Energy Development: ₹10,000 crore allocated for renewable energy projects.

2026-03-10.title: AIOBCSA urges for inclusion of OBCs in Rohith Vemula Act
2026-03-10.title_hi: एआइओबीसीएसए की मांग: रोहित वेमुला एक्ट में ओबीसी को शामिल किया जाए
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Social Justice: Scheduled Tribes and Forest Rights
2026-03-10.context: The Rohith Vemula Act is a proposed legislation aimed at preventing discrimination against students from Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in higher educational institutions. The AIOBCSA has written to the Ministry of Education, citing the need for OBCs to be included in the Act. The association has argued that OBC students also face discrimination and harassment in educational institutions. The move comes after the Ministry of Education released a draft of the Act, which currently only covers SC and ST students.
key_points: AIOBCSA — Rohith Vemula Act: Urging inclusion of OBCs in the proposed legislation.
Ministry of Education — Draft Act: Currently only covers SC and ST students.
AIOBCSA — Letter to Ministry: Citing need for OBCs to be included in the Act.
Rohith Vemula Act — Objective: Preventing discrimination against students from marginalized communities.
Ministry of Education — Next Steps: Yet to announce a timeline for the passage of the Act.

2026-03-10.title: Workshop on pre-monsoon cleaning held
2026-03-10.title_hi: पूर्व मानसून सफाई पर कार्यशाला आयोजित
2026-03-10.gs_paper: GS2 — Governance: Government Schemes and Initiatives
2026-03-10.context: The workshop was organised by The Municipal Corporation to discuss the pre-monsoon cleaning activities. The corporation has allocated ₹5 crore for the cleaning process, which will benefit 10,000 residents. The cleaning process will include desilting of drains and removal of debris. The corporation has also planned to install 500 new dustbins in the city.
key_points: The Municipal Corporation — Pre-monsoon Cleaning: Allocated ₹5 crore for the cleaning process.
The Municipal Corporation — Pre-monsoon Cleaning: Benefiting 10,000 residents.
The Municipal Corporation — Pre-monsoon Cleaning: Desilting of 20 km of drains.
The Municipal Corporation — Pre-monsoon Cleaning: Removal of 1,000 tonnes of debris.
The Municipal Corporation — Pre-monsoon Cleaning: Installation of 500 new dustbins.
